pr.title: Finish HSD_CObjGet/Set functions, fix union usages
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Finished several HSD_CObj projection boundary getter/setter matches in src/sysdolphin/baselib/cobj.c by replacing inline MUST_MATCH assembly fallbacks with C, using projection enum names instead of raw numeric cases, and correcting union field accesses for frustum and ortho projection parameters. The PR also fixed null-check logic in HSD_CObjSetLeft and HSD_CObjSetRight and made a minor DegToRad formatting cleanup.
what_changed: Replaced raw projection_type switch cases 1, 2, and 3 with PROJ_PERSPECTIVE, PROJ_FRUSTUM, and PROJ_ORTHO in HSD_CObjGetTop, HSD_CObjGetBottom, HSD_CObjGetLeft, and HSD_CObjGetRight.
Removed MUST_MATCH inline assembly implementations for HSD_CObjSetTop, HSD_CObjSetBottom, HSD_CObjSetLeft, and HSD_CObjSetRight, leaving C implementations.
Changed setter union writes so frustum cases write projection_param.frustum fields and ortho cases write projection_param.ortho fields instead of using perspective fields where inappropriate.
Corrected HSD_CObjGetBottom, HSD_CObjGetLeft, and HSD_CObjGetRight to read ortho-specific union fields for PROJ_ORTHO instead of treating ortho like frustum or perspective.
Fixed HSD_CObjSetLeft and HSD_CObjSetRight null guards from returning when cobj was non-null to returning when cobj is NULL.
Left perspective projection boundary setters as no-ops for top, bottom, left, and right, matching the switch behavior implied by the removed assembly range checks.
Adjusted DegToRad macro spacing from `*0.01745329252F` to `* 0.01745329252F`.
smart_moves: Used named projection enum constants instead of magic numbers, making the projection-type branch behavior easier to audit against struct/union layout.
Converted matched assembly-backed setters into straightforward C once the correct union members and switch cases were identified.
Preserved behavior for perspective setters by explicitly including PROJ_PERSPECTIVE cases that only break, rather than accidentally writing perspective fov/aspect for boundary setters.
Separated frustum and ortho union accesses even where the underlying offsets may overlap, improving semantic correctness without sacrificing matching.
Caught inverted null checks in SetLeft and SetRight while replacing assembly with C, preventing unreachable setter logic for valid camera objects.
decomp_lessons: For HSD_CObj projection_param, do not infer the semantic union member solely from offset equality; choose perspective, frustum, or ortho members based on projection_type.
When replacing inline asm with C, enum names can make branch ranges and intended cases clearer than raw cmpwi values.
Perspective top/bottom/left/right getters compute boundaries from near, fov, aspect, and DegToRad/tanf rather than reading boundary fields.
Frustum and ortho projection boundary getters/setters should use their own union substructs: frustum.top/bottom/left/right and ortho.top/bottom/left/right.
Check null-guard polarity carefully during asm-to-C conversion; `cmplwi r3, 0; beqlr` corresponds to `if (cobj == NULL) return;`.
Removing MUST_MATCH assembly is safest after matching both control flow and field offsets, especially for compact setters that store f1 at offsets 0x40, 0x44, 0x48, and 0x4c.
naming_conventions: Use PROJ_PERSPECTIVE, PROJ_FRUSTUM, and PROJ_ORTHO for HSD_CObj projection_type switches instead of numeric literals.
Keep HSD_CObj getter/setter names in the form HSD_CObjGetTop, HSD_CObjSetTop, HSD_CObjGetBottom, HSD_CObjSetBottom, HSD_CObjGetLeft, HSD_CObjSetLeft, HSD_CObjGetRight, and HSD_CObjSetRight.
Use projection_param.perspective for fov/aspect calculations, projection_param.frustum for frustum bounds, and projection_param.ortho for ortho bounds.
DegToRad macro formatting follows normal operator spacing: `((a) * 0.01745329252F)`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The removed setter assembly checked cobj for NULL, loaded projection_type from offset 0x50, accepted projection types in the frustum/ortho range, and stored f1 into offsets 0x40, 0x44, 0x48, or 0x4c depending on the setter.

Equivalent C switch structure used explicit cases for PROJ_PERSPECTIVE, PROJ_FRUSTUM, and PROJ_ORTHO; perspective branches break without storing while frustum/ortho branches store into the appropriate union fields.
The assembly labels lbl_80369D08, lbl_80369D10, lbl_80369DD4, lbl_80369DDC, lbl_80369EA8, lbl_80369EB0, lbl_80369F78, and lbl_80369F80 were eliminated with the matched C implementations.
Matching depended on recognizing that multiple union members share the same physical offsets, so semantically different field names can still compile to the same stfs targets.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching HSD_CObj projection_param union definitions or projection type enum values.
Search for remaining HSD_CObj getters/setters still behind MUST_MATCH assembly.
Audit other camera/object code for raw projection_type numeric cases instead of PROJ_* enum constants.
Check whether HSD_CObjGetTop later changes frustum and ortho reads from perspective.fov to explicit top fields, since this PR excerpt only shows enum-name cleanup for that getter.
